primary_metric: KS-distinguishability of Ollama (llama3.2:3b on M2) streaming signature vs Sub-exp 1 cloud anchor distributions (openai/gpt-4o-mini + anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5) at p<0.01

ks_tests._note: 2-sample KS test (scipy.stats.ks_2samp), Ollama vs each cloud anchor, per metric. p<0.01 => distinguishable.

interpretation: Hypothesis confirmed: local execution produces a distinguishably different streaming TTFT/TPS signature than any cloud-hosted endpoint. Ollama TTFT (0.179s) sits between openai's edge-fast 0.021s and anthropic's 1.099s, while Ollama TPS (43.8) is clearly below both cloud endpoints (65.4 / 83.3) — consistent with a local 3B model: no network round-trip but lower throughput than cloud-hosted inference.
anchor_source: Sub-exp 1 (B13) raw data: openai n=1300, anthropic n=1300 valid (status==ok, ttft_s+tps present)
early_close_note: Closed 2026-06-02 after 18 fires (Day-3 final 22:00 UTC fire skipped). 800 valid = 2.1x the 375 nominal-valid expectation and 4x the 200 kill floor; an additional 50-call fire cannot change a verdict where every anchor p-value is already << 0.01.
